fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arizona Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer pharmacist license to arizona, pharmacist compact arizona, arizona pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Arizona Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.
board.mailing_address: 1740 W. Adams St., Suite 200, Phoenix, AZ 85007

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Arizona.

steps.title: Create NABP e-Profile and Register for Exams
steps.description: Establish an e-Profile account with the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) and register for the NAPLEX and MPJE examinations.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts to NABP
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your ACPE-accredited college of pharmacy to be sent directly to NABP.

steps.title: Create an AZ BOP e-licensing Portal Account
steps.description: Go to the AZ BOP e-licensing portal at https://elicense.azbop.com/ and create a new user account.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log into your AZ BOP e-licensing portal account, complete the Pharmacist Application, and submit it along with the required application fee and your MPJE score.

steps.title: Submit Fingerprint Card
steps.description: Obtain a fingerprint card and submit it to the Arizona Board of Pharmacy for a criminal background check as instructed by the board.

steps.title: NABP Score Transfer
steps.description: NABP will electronically transfer your NAPLEX scores to the Arizona Board of Pharmacy upon completion of the exam.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Arizona pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Arizona have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Arizona?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Arizona
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Arizona, you must create an account and submit your application through the AZ BOP e-licensing portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, click the 'New Users: Create an Account' link on the AZ BOP e-licensing portal homepage and follow the prompts. Once registered, log in and select the appropriate pharmacist application.
